question: Will CPI rise more than 0.9% in March 2026?
reasoning: Multiple sources indicate March 2026 CPI will show significant monthly increases due to an Iran war driving energy price spikes. BofA forecasts 0.9% monthly CPI increase (0.91% unrounded) due to 10.6% monthly energy price jump. Kiplinger expects 0.8% monthly headline CPI. The March data will be released April 10 at 8:30 AM ET, making this a near-certain outcome given the war's energy price impacts.
key_factors: Iran war driving energy price spikes in March 2026
BofA economists forecast exactly 0.91% monthly CPI increase
Energy prices expected to jump 10.6% month-over-month
Report releases April 10, so market resolution is imminent
Recent February CPI showed 0.3% monthly increase already accelerating
risks: Seasonal adjustments could reduce the final reported number
Energy price spike might be smaller than forecasted
Core CPI expected to be softer which could offset energy gains
BLS revision processes could affect final calculations
